title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward has 10 hits and 12 stolen bases in 12 games, leading all Red Sox in steals during spring training.
Max Ferguson leads Red Sox in RBIs with seven, thanks to a grand slam, and has a .821 OPS in 17 at bats.
Tayron Guerrero has pitched six relief innings, striking out six and allowing one earned run, showing potential for bullpen role.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second in 5-on-5 goals for Bruins with 13 this season.
Arvidsson, a pending free agent, remains crucial despite trade potential due to his scoring and leadership.
Bruins consider Arvidsson a potential short-term solution in their retooling efforts for future seasons.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots traded center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears, likely prompting a reshuffle of their offensive line.
Jared Wilson is expected to move from left guard to center, increasing the need for a new left guard.
Potential targets for the Patriots include Isaac Seumalo, David Edwards, Joel Bitonio, Elgton Jenkins, and draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Make-A-Wish Foundation children.
Perry, from Stockton, California, aims to honor and raise funds for Wish children in Massachusetts and Rhode Island.
Over 15 years, Perry has met many Wish children, gaining inspiration from their resilience and hope.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are predicted to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe for a three-year, $48 million contract, according to ESPN.
Boye Mafe, 27, recorded 44 pressures and a 17.4% pass rush win rate with the Seahawks in 2025.
Patriots unlikely to sign Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce, who is projected to join the Chargers for $80 million over three years.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at the House of Blues in Boston on March 6, debuting politically charged songs with satirical lyrics.
The setlist included new songs like 'Sometimes You Bomb Iran' alongside covers of Black Sabbath and Radiohead classics.
Welles' performance featured 20 songs, highlighting his sharp commentary on social issues such as war and political figures.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a 387-foot home run, contributing three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 victory over Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida's performance capped a strong inning with homers by Shohei Ohtani and Seiya Suzuki, helping Japan improve to 2-0 in pool play.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in a Dodgers uniform while on a trip to Japan with his wife.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, is running her first Boston Marathon to support Mass. Eye and Ear.
Aboujaoude-Quirk is a Boston native from Roslindale and currently teaches in Boston Public Schools.
Mass. Eye and Ear recognized Aboujaoude-Quirk's eye conditions, which are now under control with regular visits.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team in August 2025 alongside Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler joined ESPN in 2000 after a 10-year NBA career and is now a color analyst for the NBA Finals.
Legler fills a role left by Doc Rivers and JJ Redick, who left ESPN for NBA coaching positions in 2024.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Cooper Flagg scored 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists for the Mavericks at TD Garden against the Celtics.
Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists after a 298-day absence.
Flagg received strong support from Maine fans, marking his NBA debut at TD Garden amid Tatum's celebrated return.
